turns.user_message: Honestly, I'm just not sure I believe all the hype about how bad smoking is for me. I mean, I'm healthy, I exercise, I eat well... I just don't think it's that big of a deal. And when I do try to quit, I get so anxious and irritable. It's like, I know I'm supposed to be doing something good for myself, but it feels like torture. And then there's the stress at work. I don't know how I'll deal with it without cigarettes.

Therapist: Hello, how are you? Welcome to EDosthi. I'm here to help you with tobacco cessation. Would you like to tell me about your tobacco use?

Patient: Yeah, I guess so. I've been smoking for about 10 years now. I've tried to quit a few times, but it never sticks. I'm just really stressed out with my job, and cigarettes help me cope. But I know I need to quit. My friends and family are always on my case about it.

Therapist: I understand. It sounds like you're feeling a bit stuck. Can you tell me more about what makes it hard for you to quit? What are some of the biggest challenges you face when you try to stop smoking?

Patient: Honestly, I'm just not sure I believe all the hype about how bad smoking is for me. I mean, I'm healthy, I exercise, I eat well... I just don't think it's that big of a deal. And when I do try to quit, I get so anxious and irritable. It's like, I know I'm supposed to be doing something good for myself, but it feels like torture. And then there's the stress at work. I don't know how I'll deal with it without cigarettes.

step7_response: It sounds like you're questioning the severity of smoking's impact on your health, which is understandable given your habits and lifestyle. Can you imagine how your family would feel if you were to quit smoking, and what kind of support they could offer you in this process?

turns.user_message: I don't know, I just feel like I'm an exception or something. I'm not like those people who smoke a pack a day and have heart attacks in their 40s. I'm active, I take care of myself... I just don't think it'll happen to me. But at the same time, I know it's stupid to think that way. Deep down, I know I'm probably just in denial.

[Recent Conversation]
Therapist: Hello, how are you? Welcome to EDosthi. I'm here to help you with tobacco cessation. Would you like to tell me about your tobacco use?

Patient: Yeah, I guess so. I've been smoking for about 10 years now. I've tried to quit a few times, but it never sticks. I'm just really stressed out with my job, and cigarettes help me cope. But I know I need to quit. My friends and family are always on my case about it.

Therapist: I understand. It sounds like you're feeling a bit stuck. Can you tell me more about what makes it hard for you to quit? What are some of the biggest challenges you face when you try to stop smoking?

Patient: Honestly, I'm just not sure I believe all the hype about how bad smoking is for me. I mean, I'm healthy, I exercise, I eat well... I just don't think it's that big of a deal. And when I do try to quit, I get so anxious and irritable. It's like, I know I'm supposed to be doing something good for myself, but it feels like torture. And then there's the stress at work. I don't know how I'll deal with it without cigarettes.

Therapist: I see. It sounds like you're struggling with some skepticism about the health risks, and also worried about managing your stress and withdrawal symptoms. Can you tell me more about what you mean by "healthy smoker"? What makes you think you're not at risk for serious health problems?

Patient: I don't know, I just feel like I'm an exception or something. I'm not like those people who smoke a pack a day and have heart attacks in their 40s. I'm active, I take care of myself... I just don't think it'll happen to me. But at the same time, I know it's stupid to think that way. Deep down, I know I'm probably just in denial.

[REASONING]

Therapist:
step7_response: It sounds like you're feeling like you're in a bit of denial about the risks of smoking, but also wanting to hold onto the idea that you're an exception. Can you think of a time when you felt like you were in control of your smoking, and what was different about that situation?

turns.user_message: I guess I need to find some other ways to cope with stress that actually work. Maybe some relaxation techniques or something. But it's hard because cigarettes are just so... easy. I don't know, maybe I just need to believe in myself more. That I can do it.

Therapist: Rachel, it takes a lot of courage to acknowledge that denial. It sounds like you're starting to recognize the risks, even if you don't fully believe them yet. Let's focus on the stress management piece for a minute. What do you think would need to happen for you to feel confident that you can manage your stress without cigarettes?

Patient: I guess I need to find some other ways to cope with stress that actually work. Maybe some relaxation techniques or something. But it's hard because cigarettes are just so... easy. I don't know, maybe I just need to believe in myself more. That I can do it.
